why.sectionDescription: Industry-leading features that set us apart

hero.title: Revolutionizing Global Maritime Trade
hero.description: ONTIME RESOURCES LIMITED is at the forefront of maritime technology, connecting the world through innovative cargo solutions.

story.sectionDescription: The story behind Ontime Maritime and our mission to modernize global shipping
story.label: Our Story
story.title: Building the Digital Backbone of Modern Maritime Logistics
story.p1: Founded in 2020, Ontime Maritime emerged from a simple observation: the maritime industry was decades behind in digital transformation. While other sectors embraced technology, cargo shipping remained trapped in paperwork and inefficiency.
story.p2: Our founders — veterans of both global shipping and Silicon Valley — saw an opportunity to bridge this gap. They brought together maritime experts, software engineers, and logistics professionals with one mission: modernize how cargo moves across the world.
story.p3: Today, Ontime Maritime supports over 5,000 companies in 150+ countries, processing millions of containers annually and powering critical supply chains worldwide.

impact.sectionDescription: Measurable impact across global maritime operations
impact.stat1Desc: Active presence across major global ports

purpose.sectionDescription: The mission and vision guiding Ontime Maritime's impact on global trade
purpose.missionDesc: To democratize access to global maritime trade by providing cutting-edge technology that makes shipping faster, more transparent, and more efficient for businesses of all sizes. We believe every company deserves enterprise-level logistics capabilities.
purpose.visionDesc: To become the world's most trusted maritime technology platform, where every shipment is tracked, every document is digital, and every transaction is seamless. We envision a future where global trade is accessible to all.
purpose.vision2: Platform in every major port worldwide

values.sectionDescription: The principles that guide everything we do
values.excellenceDesc: We strive for excellence in every feature, every interaction, and every customer experience.
values.collaborationDesc: We believe in the power of partnership and building connections across the maritime ecosystem.
values.innovationDesc: We continuously push boundaries to deliver cutting-edge solutions for modern maritime challenges.
values.integrityDesc: We operate with transparency, honesty, and accountability in all our business dealings.

team.sectionDescription: Meet the experts driving maritime innovation

ports.title: Complete Nigerian Port Coverage
ports.subtitle: From deep-sea terminals to inland waterways and dry ports — Ontime Resources Limited operates across every major cargo gateway in Nigeria.

map.title: Port Network Map
map.subtitle: Click any pin to view port details

seaportsSection.title: 7 Major Seaports
seaportsSection.subtitle: Ontime Resources Limited maintains active clearing operations at every licensed seaport in Nigeria.

inlandSection.title: Inland Waterway
inlandSection.subtitle: River-based cargo movement connecting coastal ports to Nigeria's southeast hinterland.
inlandSection.whyText: The River Niger route cuts haulage costs by up to 40% for Southeast-bound cargo compared to road-only transport.
inlandSection.whyDetail: Ontime Resources Limited is one of the few Lagos-based freight agents offering active inland waterway coordination — a critical advantage for clients in Anambra, Enugu, Imo, and Abia States.

dryPortsSection.title: Our Inland Port Network
dryPortsSection.explanation: Dry ports extend Nigeria's port capacity deep into the country's interior. Ontime Resources Limited provides clearing and forwarding services at all major Nigerian dry ports, enabling importers and exporters in landlocked states to clear cargo without travelling to the seaport.

cta.title: Operating at a port not listed here?
cta.description: Our network is expanding and our team can coordinate customs clearing at any Nigerian facility upon client request. Contact us and we will make it work.
cta.slogan: "Where Cargo Meets Technology" · NIMASA Licence SA261009 · Active & Compliant

apapa.name: Lagos Port Complex, Apapa
apapa.capability: Primary operational hub. Full customs clearing, FCL and LCL container handling, demurrage dispute management, Form M processing, destination inspection coordination, PAAR, SON and NAFDAC waivers. Fastest turnaround at Nigeria's busiest port.

tin-can.name: Tin Can Island Port
tin-can.capability: Dedicated container terminal. Import clearing for consumer goods, vehicles, machinery, and bulk cargo. Vehicle importation specialist. Consolidated LCL shipment management with direct road access to Lagos industrial corridor.

lekki.name: Lekki Deep Sea Port
lekki.capability: Nigeria's largest and newest deep-sea port. Ultra-large vessel capability. Project cargo, high-volume FCL operations, heavy industrial equipment importation. Positioned for the next decade of Nigerian trade growth.

onne.name: Onne Port Complex
onne.capability: Specialist oil and gas cargo clearing. Hazardous materials documentation, OPAL/OGSL processing, industrial equipment importation, project logistics coordination. Primary gateway for the Niger Delta energy sector.

port-harcourt.name: Port Harcourt Port
port-harcourt.capability: General cargo, agro-commodity exports, petroleum product documentation, multipurpose cargo handling. Serves Rivers State, Bayelsa, and the broader Niger Delta trade corridor.

warri.name: Warri Port
warri.capability: Bulk cargo, petroleum products, agro-exports. Gateway for Delta and Edo State consignees. Timber, cocoa, and palm produce export documentation and coordination.

calabar.name: Calabar Port
calabar.capability: Nigeria's premier agro-export port. Cocoa, sesame, timber, and commodity export documentation. Phytosanitary certificates, NEPC registration, certificate of origin. Gateway to Southeast and South-South Nigeria.

onitsha.name: Onitsha Inland Port
onitsha.capability: River Niger cargo route connecting Lagos to Southeast Nigeria. Cost-effective bulk movement alternative to road haulage. Serves Anambra, Enugu, Imo, and Abia States. Unique capability not offered by most Lagos-based clearing agents.

kano-dry-port.name: Kano Dry Port (Inland Container Depot)
kano-dry-port.capability: Customs clearing for cargo transferred by rail or road from Lagos and Apapa. Container examination, duty assessment, and final release coordination. Covers manufactured goods, machinery, raw materials, and agro-inputs for Northern Nigeria.

kaduna-dry-port.name: Kaduna Dry Port
kaduna-dry-port.capability: Clearing of rail-transferred containers from Lagos Port. Industrial equipment, machinery, and manufactured goods destined for North-Central Nigeria. Full coordination with NRC (Nigerian Railway Corporation) logistics.

maiduguri-dry-port.name: Maiduguri Dry Port
maiduguri-dry-port.capability: Clearing and forwarding for humanitarian cargo, agricultural inputs, manufactured goods, and trade with Cameroon, Chad, and Niger Republic. Northeast Nigeria's primary inland clearance facility.

funtua-dry-port.name: Funtua Dry Port
funtua-dry-port.capability: Agro-export documentation, cotton and commodity clearing, agro-input imports. Specialist in North-West agricultural trade corridor documentation. Serves Katsina, Kebbi, Sokoto, and Zamfara States.

heipang-dry-port.name: Heipang Dry Port
heipang-dry-port.capability: Solid mineral exports, agricultural commodities, general cargo. Documentation for tin, columbite, and Jos Plateau mineral trade exports. Serves Plateau, Nasarawa, Benue, and Taraba States.

ibadan-dry-port.name: Ibadan Dry Port (Inland Container Terminal)
ibadan-dry-port.capability: Cargo overflow from Lagos ports. Textile and manufacturing imports, agro-exports, consumer goods. Direct connection to Lagos Port Complex via expressway and planned rail corridor. Serves Oyo, Osun, Ekiti, and Kwara States.

credentials.title: Verified Credentials & Professional Certifications
credentials.subtitle: Ontime Resources Limited operates under government-verified licensing, internationally recognised academic qualifications, and full regulatory compliance — credentials that most Nigerian clearing agents simply do not hold.
credentials.trustStatement: All credentials listed below are current, active, and verifiable.
credentials.trustLink: NIMASA Licence SA261009 can be checked directly at www.nimasa.gov.ng

hero.title: NIMASA Registration Licence
hero.issuer: Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency
hero.authority: Issued under the Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency Act 2007 and Merchant Shipping Act 2007.

qualifications.sectionSubtitle: Our leadership combines legal expertise in international maritime law with hands-on port operations experience — a combination that directly protects every client instruction we manage.
qualifications.whyMatters: Why This Matters For Your Cargo

llm-maritime-law.title: LLM International Commercial and Maritime Law
llm-maritime-law.relevance: Every cargo instruction handled by Ontime Resources is managed by a team led by a qualified maritime lawyer. This means your contracts are reviewed correctly, your claims are handled professionally, and your rights are protected at every stage — under Nigerian and international maritime law.

cma-conference.title: CMA Shipping Conference Attendance Certificate
cma-conference.meta: 2009 · One of the world's most prestigious annual shipping events
cma-conference.relevance: The CMA Shipping Conference gathers global shipping executives, shipowners, financiers, and logistics leaders annually. Attendance demonstrates active participation in the international shipping industry at its highest level — not just local market knowledge.

cac.title: Certificate of Incorporation
cac.issuer: Corporate Affairs Commission, Federal Republic of Nigeria
cac.registeredUnderValue: CAMA — Companies and Allied Matters Act

ncs.title: Nigeria Customs Service Compliance
ncs.statement: Ontime Resources Limited is a recognised customs clearing agent operating in full compliance with Nigeria Customs Service regulations, tariff classifications, and destination inspection procedures at all Nigerian ports of entry.

benefits.sectionTitle: What Our Credentials Mean For You

legalProtection.title: Your Cargo is Legally Protected
legalProtection.text: Our NIMASA licence and LLM in maritime law means every cargo instruction is managed under full legal compliance. If disputes arise with terminal operators, shipping lines, or customs officials, our qualified leadership knows exactly how to protect your interests under Nigerian and international maritime law.

verifiedAgent.title: You Are Working With a Verified Agent
verifiedAgent.text: NIMASA Licence SA261009 is publicly verifiable at www.nimasa.gov.ng. You are not taking our word for it — you can check our licence status right now, in real time, before you instruct us with a single container. Most Nigerian clearing agents cannot offer this.

international.title: International Standard Operations
international.text: Our MD's LLM from a UK university and participation in the CMA Shipping Conference in the USA means our operations are benchmarked against international standards, not just local practices. You receive the same professional standard you would expect from a clearing agent in London, Singapore, or Shanghai.

transparency.title: Verification & Transparency Statement
transparency.text: We believe transparency is the foundation of every lasting business relationship. That is why every credential shown on this page is real, current, and independently verifiable. We do not display expired licences. We do not inflate our qualifications. We do not make claims we cannot substantiate.
transparency.facts: NIMASA Licence SA261009 was issued on 25th March 2026 and is valid until 24th March 2027. Our LLM was awarded by Swansea University, United Kingdom in 2010. Our company is registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission of Nigeria under RC 7543149. These are facts. Check them.
